 TROPESS PAN Level 2 Summary Product User Guide

1 Introduction

1.1

Overview and Document Scope

This document is to be used as a Quick start user guide for using the TROPESS PAN Level 2

Summary Product Files.

1.2

Dataset Description

This user’s guide describes the TROPESS Level 2 Summary product files for peroxyacetyl 

nitrate (PAN). 

Product Information
Parameters
Data Product Provenance
Approximate file size
Spatial coverage

Description
 See Table 2, in section 2.1
Refer to ReadMe document, section 1.3.4 Algorithm Version
16MB CrIS
Regular collections have global coverage: 
Nominal latitude range: 70 N to 50 S
Nominal longitude range: -180 to 180

Temporal coverage

Special collections: Spatial coverage varies by collection
Each L2 Summary file contains 1 day of data

File format

netcdf

Vertical sensitivity

Data quality

PAN retrievals have sensitivity between approximately 800 hPa and the 
tropopause.  Only a vertically integrated quantity should be used, as values at 
individual pressures do not contain independent information. This file contains 
vertically integrated values.

The data have undergone a pre-quality check, which involves checks for retrieval 
convergence. There are no checks for clouds or land versus ocean as we do not find
these to substantively affect the quality of the retrieval as long as the retrieval has 
converged.  Note that retrievals over thick clouds will not be sensitive to PAN 
below the clouds.

Observational uncertainty

Uncertainties on vertically-averaged PAN values between 825.4 and 215.4 hPa are 
estimated at 0.17 ppb for a single sounding.

Validation Stage

Retrieval Levels
FillValues

Stage 2 according to NASA guidelines https://science.nasa.gov/earth-science/earth-
science-data/data-maturity-levels
16 levels: from surface to top-of-atmosphere 
-999

Retrieval terminology 

Retrieval terminology is defined in TES ATBD TROPESS_ATBDv1.1.pdf 

3

 TROPESS PAN Level 2 Summary Product User Guide

(nasa.gov)

1.3

Filename

The Summary Products adhere to the following filename convention:

[ProjectID]_[Instrument-
Platform]_[ProductLevel]_[ProductType]_[ProductName]_[DateStamp]_ 
[AlgorithmName]_[AlgorithmVersion]_[ProcessingStrategy]_[FormatVersion].nc 

Example:

TROPESS_CrIS-SNPP_L2_Summary_PAN_20200912_MUSES_R1p11_FS_F0p1.nc

3 References

Citing these data

3.1
Please cite the following references if you intend to use these data:

1. Payne, V. H. et al., Satellite measurements of peroxyacetyl nitrate from the Cross-

track Infrared Sounder: Comparison with ATom aircraft measurements, in 
preparation for submission to Atmos. Meas. Techniques, 2021

2. Juncosa Calahorrano, J. et al., Evolution of PAN in wildfire smoke plumes detected 

by the Cross-track Infrared Sounder (CrIS) over the western US during summer 2018,
Geophys. Res. Lett., accepted, 2021 

3.2

Additional references

The following references were also used in the development of this documentation: 

1. Payne, V. H. et al, Satellite observations of peroxyacetyl nitrate from the Tropospheric 

Emission Spectrometer, Atmos. Meas. Tech., 7, 3737-3749 (2014)

2. Bowman, K.W. et al., Tropospheric Emission Spectrometer: Retrieval Method and Error 

Analysis, IEEE Trans. Geosci. Remote Sensing, 44, 1297- 1307, May 2006.
3. Rodgers, C. D., and B. J. Connor (2003), Intercomparisons of remote sounding 
instruments, J. Geophys. Res., 108(D3), 4116, doi:10.1029/2002JD002299

4. Validation Summary

8

 TROPESS PAN Level 2 Summary Product User Guide

See L2 Standard Products User’s Guide for Validation Summary.

5. Quality flags

The Quality flags applied to this data are (see archival product for these fields):

RadianceResidualRMS < 5.0 

abs(RadianceResidualMean) < 3.5 

ResidualNormFinal < 5.0 

Desert_Emiss_QA > 0.925  (version 1.16) and Desert_Emiss_QA > 0.95 (previous versions)

SurfaceTemperature > 265 

TropopausePressure < CloudTopPressure + 20

 radiance_residual_stdev_change > -0.15

Appendix A.  Bias correction and quality flags

The data in the L2 Summary Products have been pre-filtered for quality. No bias correction is 
currently applied to the PAN values. See L2 Standard Product User’s guides for definitions.

Appendix B.  Retrieval levels

The table below contains the nominal retrieval levels.  For each sounding, the surface 
pressure level is inserted into the retrieval levels set. Any retrieval levels below the surface 
pressure level are omitted.
